title: What am I afraid will happen if I stop watching?
subtitle: A journal prompt set for examining the fear underneath the vigilance that won't let you switch off.
excerpt: A journal prompt set from Day 18 of the SLEEP program. Five prompts for naming what the nervous system believes will happen if you stop watching, and whether that belief is still serving you.
sourceNotes: Adapted from Day 18 daily reflection in SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive (Rebecca J Page, 2025), "Safe to switch off."

practice.whenToUse: Use when evenings are hard to wind down, or when you notice you stay 'on' long after the day's tasks are done.

content.text: A journal prompt set from Day 18 of the SLEEP program. The prompts look directly at the belief driving the vigil rather than trying to reason it away.
New to journalling as a practice? Read
What am I afraid will happen if I switch off completely tonight?
Write the specific fear, not the rational rebuttal. What does the nervous system believe?
Where did I first learn that staying 'on' kept things safe?
Write the source if it comes. A person, a situation, a period of time when vigilance was needed.
What has actually gone wrong on nights when I rested well?
Write the evidence. Not the hypothetical — what has actually happened.
What would I hand to 'something larger than me' if I could — whatever that phrase means to me?
Write the specific list. Name what you would give over if the night watch wasn't yours to keep.
What would switching off fully tonight give me, if it were completely safe to do it?
Write the specific gains: rest, repair, clarity tomorrow. Let yourself want them.
Adapted from Day 18 daily reflection in SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
